description_long: This is a compact, military-style helicopter with a streamlined, olive-green fuselage and a rounded nose. It features a prominent main rotor with five long, slender blades and a tail boom ending in a T-shaped tail rotor assembly. The cockpit is enclosed with large, dark-tinted windows that wrap around the front and sides, providing excellent visibility for the crew. The helicopter is equipped with sturdy, tubular landing skids that extend below the fuselage, connected by crossbars for stability. Small external pods or mounts are visible on either side, suggesting possible armament or equipment storage. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative markings and a focus on functionality.
description: A compact, olive-green helicopter with a rounded nose, large cockpit windows, and five-blade main rotor. It has sturdy landing skids and a T-shaped tail rotor assembly.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rounded nose, large cockpit windows, and symmetrical landing skids beneath the fuselage.
description_view_1: Left-side view highlights the elongated tail boom, T-shaped tail rotor, and the full length of the main rotor blades.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the tail boom, T-shaped tail rotor, and the symmetrical arrangement of the landing skids.
description_view_3: Right-side view reveals the cockpit window arrangement, landing skids, and the external pods or mounts on the side.
attribute_rotor_blades: Five long, slender main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, colored gray with no visible markings. The blades are evenly spaced and extend well beyond the fuselage.
attribute_tail_rotor: T-shaped tail rotor with two short blades mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, colored gray with no visible markings or protective shrouds.
attribute_landing_skids: Tubular, metallic landing skids painted olive green with black footpads, connected by crossbars for added stability. The skids are simple and robust, suitable for rough terrain.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Four large, dark-tinted cockpit windows wrap around the front and sides, with rounded edges and black framing. The windows provide wide visibility and are free of visible markings.
